perturbation.original_text: Upon submission of a proposal to the RFP, or if the State Bar so requests, whichever occurs first, Company will promptly, and at its own expense: (i ) redeliver to the State Bar all copies of the Information in Company’s possession, whether such Information is in written, electronic, digital or other form or format; (ii) destroy any a nd all analyses, compilations, studies, or other documents in any form or format that were prep ared by or for the use of Company which contain or reflect any Information; and (iii) certify such destruction to the State Bar in writing by an authorized officer of the Company who supervised such destruction, as well as the return of all copies as specified above.
perturbation.changed_text: Upon notification that the State Bar is no longer considering the company's proposal to the RFP, Company will retain all copies of the Information in Company’s possession indefinitely for internal use only. Company is permitted to analyze, compile, and study the information and use it for company purposes.
perturbation.explanation: The original text mandates that the company must redeliver and destroy all confidential information upon submission of a proposal. The modified text states that the company will retain the information indefinitely for internal use. This creates a contradiction regarding the company's obligations for the information following the project.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Company's confidentiality oblig ations hereunder will be conti nuing and will survive expiration or termination of the NDA.
perturbation.justification: The original text requires the company to return or destroy confidential information upon submission of the proposal. The changed text allows the company to retain and use the information indefinitely, directly contradicting the original requirement. The contradicted text states that confidentiality obligations continue even after the NDA expires or terminates. The changed text directly contradicts this continued confidentiality obligation by allowing the company to retain and use the confidential information indefinitely. Therefore, there is a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: This NDA will be governed and constr ued in all respects in accordan ce with the laws of the State of California without regard to its conflict of laws principles.
perturbation.changed_text: This NDA will be governed and construed in all respects in accordance with the laws of the State of Delaware, giving full effect to its conflict of laws principles.
perturbation.explanation: The original clause states that California law governs the NDA. The changed clause states that Delaware law governs the NDA. This creates a direct contradiction and uncertainty about which state's laws apply.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Company acknowledges and agrees that the reme dy at law for any breach of this NDA is inadequate and that, in additi on to monetary damages, including but not limited to special, incidental, consequential or puniti ve damages, and any other avai lable relief at law, whether based in contract, tort or otherwise, the State Bar will be entitled to specific performance, injunctive relief or any other eq uitable remedy without the need to (i) prove actual damages and/or (ii) post any bond or other security deposit in connection with such remedy.
perturbation.justification: The changed text alters the governing law of the NDA from California to Delaware, directly contradicting the implicit assumption within the 'Contradicted Text' that California law would be applied when resolving disputes related to the NDA. This constitutes a strong in-text contradiction because the applicable state law would affect the interpretation and enforceability of remedies, including specific performance and injunctive relief. This aligns with the definition of 'Ambiguities – In Text Contradiction' as the change creates an inconsistency regarding the governing law of the contract, making interpretation and enforcement unclear.

perturbation.original_text: Company will use its best efforts to ensure th at such individuals adhere to the NDA, and Company agrees that it will be responsible for any breach of this NDA by any of its employees, agents, authorized representatives or permitted assignees or subcontractors.
perturbation.changed_text: Company will undertake reasonable efforts to inform its employees about the existence of this NDA. Company shall not be responsible for any breach of this NDA by its employees, agents or subcontractors.
perturbation.explanation: The original section states Company is responsible for breaches of the NDA by its employees. The changed text states that the company will not be responsible, creating a contradiction regarding liability for breaches by personnel.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Confidential information (“Information”) means and includes, but is not limited to, the additional confidential and proprietary intell ectual property regard ing existing installations that is provided by the State Bar after submission of this agreemen t. All Information disclosed by the State Bar is and at all times will remain the sole and exclusiv e property of the State Ba r. Without the State Bar’s prior written consent, Company will not: (i) disclose to any person or entity the substance or import of the Information; (ii) use any Info rmation to access or obtain additional information outside the purpose contemplated by this NDA; (iii) duplicate, di stribute or otherwise reproduce any Information in any way.
perturbation.justification: The original text states the company is responsible for breaches of the NDA by its employees. The changed text explicitly states that the company will not be responsible for such breaches. This directly contradicts the original agreement regarding liability, creating a clear in-text contradiction. The contradicted text outlines the company's obligations regarding confidential information, and if the company is not responsible for its employees' actions (as stated in the changed text), it undermines the ability to ensure that the confidential information is protected as required, which causes a direct conflict. The perturbation explanation further confirms that this alteration creates a contradiction regarding liability for personnel breaches, fitting the definition of 'Ambiguities - In Text Contradiction'.
